name: Madrid
summary: <cite index="2-1,5-12">Madrid holds a Level 2 travel advisory for increased caution due to terrorism risks, but consistently ranks among the top five safest countries in Europe with one of the highest public safety ratings in the EU.</cite> <cite index="14-2,16-6,20-1">The Spanish capital is considered one of the safest megacities globally and generally very safe compared to other major capitals.</cite>
quickVerdict: Madrid is exceptionally safe for tourists, ranking as one of Europe's safest capitals with very low violent crime rates. <cite index="15-6">Spain has a homicide rate of 0.64 per 100,000 population, making it the 6th lowest among 30 European countries.</cite> The main concerns for visitors are petty crimes like pickpocketing in tourist areas and occasional scams, but these are easily avoided with basic precautions. The city's excellent public transport system, strong police presence in tourist zones, and vibrant nightlife culture make it particularly welcoming for solo travelers and women.

neighborhoods.name: Salamanca
neighborhoods.description: <cite index="40-30,40-31">Madrid's luxury district known for upscale shopping and fine dining, this area is one of the safest in the city with a quiet and affluent atmosphere.</cite> Well-patrolled with excellent lighting and minimal crime incidents.

neighborhoods.name: Retiro
neighborhoods.description: Home to the famous Retiro Park and close to major museums, this central area is heavily patrolled and well-maintained. Popular with families and considered very safe day and night.

neighborhoods.name: Malasaña
neighborhoods.description: <cite index="40-17">This trendy neighborhood is typically bustling until late into the night</cite>, with a strong police presence and vibrant nightlife that provides natural safety through busy streets and constant activity.

neighborhoods.name: Central Madrid (Sol/Gran Vía)
neighborhoods.description: <cite index="13-12,13-13">Madrid's central district leads in incident numbers with 1,564 police interventions recorded, though this is largely due to high tourist volume and non-violent thefts in crowded areas.</cite> Well-policed but requires vigilance for pickpockets.

neighborhoods.name: Lavapiés
neighborhoods.description: <cite index="40-34,40-35">A multicultural area offering a unique, authentic experience with a slightly edgier vibe but still safe for solo travelers who appreciate diverse cultures and artistic spaces.</cite> Exercise normal urban caution, especially at night.

neighborhoods.name: Villaverde
neighborhoods.description: <cite index="13-19,13-20">Located in southern Madrid, this district recorded 199 incidents and includes areas facing social and economic challenges, which can contribute to minor offences, though serious violent crime remains limited.</cite> Avoid late night visits and stay alert.

scams.name: Lucky Rosemary Fortune Telling
scams.description: <cite index="21-8,21-9,21-10">Elderly women in tourist areas hand you rosemary twigs and tell your fortune, then demand payment between €5-€20. They may create a public scene if you refuse.</cite>
scams.howToAvoid: <cite index="27-12,25-37">Firmly decline any items offered by strangers, avoid eye contact, and keep walking. Don't accept anything placed in your hands.</cite>

scams.name: Fake Police Document Check
scams.description: <cite index="21-11,21-12,21-13,21-14">Scammers with realistic badges approach tourists claiming to be undercover police, asking to check wallets for counterfeit money, then steal cash before returning the wallet.</cite>
scams.howToAvoid: <cite index="21-15,21-16,21-17">Stay calm, ask to see their badge again, and demand the inspection take place at the nearest police station. Real police don't conduct street wallet inspections.</cite>

scams.name: Restaurant Overcharging
scams.description: <cite index="21-22,21-23,21-24">Restaurants convince tourists to order 'daily specials' from chalkboards without prices, then charge inflated amounts when the bill arrives.</cite>
scams.howToAvoid: <cite index="21-25,21-26">Always ask for prices before ordering daily specials, and request both Spanish and English menus to check for price discrepancies.</cite>

scams.name: Shell Game / Three Card Monte
scams.description: <cite index="21-27,21-28,21-29">Street performers use cups and a pea to trick tourists into betting money, often with accomplices in the crowd to encourage participation and create distractions.</cite>
scams.howToAvoid: Never participate in street gambling games. Avoid crowds watching these games as spectators are often part of the scam. Walk away immediately.

soloFemale.overview: <cite index="31-1,31-2,38-15,38-16">Madrid is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers, described as 'the safest city I've ever been to' by experienced solo travelers.</cite> <cite index="37-1,37-2,37-10">The city is generally safe for female travelers including solo visitors, with standard urban awareness particularly at night keeping risk low.</cite> <cite index="33-3,33-4,33-5">Solo female travelers should be cautious of unwanted attention in busier nightlife areas and avoid walking alone late at night on less crowded streets, preferring well-lit, populated routes.</cite>
tips: <cite index="38-27,38-28,38-30">Street harassment exists but is generally less menacing than other countries - expect occasional whistling or comments like 'qué guapa' but without following, threats, or aggressive behavior.</cite>
<cite index="40-16,40-17">Stay in busy areas, especially after dark - neighborhoods like La Latina, Malasaña, and Salamanca are typically bustling until late into the night.</cite>

<cite index="33-5,33-6">Watch your drink and only accept drinks from people you trust, as instances of drink spiking have been reported. Consider joining group tours or activities for added safety.</cite>

nightSafety.overview: <cite index="20-3,20-4,20-15,20-16">Madrid is safe at night, especially compared to other major capitals, with violent crime being rare and the city center full of life. The lively atmosphere contributes to safety as you're rarely alone on streets, even late at night.</cite> <cite index="16-15,16-32,16-36">Muggings are uncommon in central Madrid, with busy tourist spots being heavily patrolled, well-lit, and filled with visitors, making them very secure for solo travelers and women even at night if sticking to populated areas.</cite>
tips: <cite index="41-2,41-3,41-29,41-30">Madrid is very safe at night with 'Búhos' (night buses) providing a reliable and cheap alternative to taxis after the Metro closes at 1:30 AM.</cite>

<cite index="20-13,20-14,20-15">Take advantage of Madrid's late dining culture - locals eat dinner after 9 PM and neighborhoods like Sol, Gran Vía, Chueca, Malasaña remain busy and filled with people long after sundown.</cite>

<cite index="31-14,31-15,31-16">Practice common sense and street smarts - keep an eye on surroundings, stay alert in crowded places, and steer clear of poorly lit streets when alone after dark.</cite>

transport.metro: <cite index="41-35,43-4,45-5,45-6">The Madrid Metro is the fastest, most reliable, clean and efficient way to get around the city, serving as the most convenient transportation option.</cite> <cite index="43-19,43-20">With 293 kilometers of tracks and 302 stations, it's one of Europe's largest metro systems, getting you within walking distance of almost any destination.</cite> <cite index="50-25,50-26,50-32">Public transport in Madrid is considered one of the best in Europe - clean, reliable, and very safe with signs in both Spanish and English.</cite>
transport.rideshare: <cite index="47-1,47-2,47-7,47-8">Uber operates in Madrid and works just like in other cities globally, though it's less popular among residents who favor other apps like Cabify.</cite> <cite index="43-25,43-26,43-1,43-2">Ride-sharing apps like Uber, Cabify, or Bolt provide fixed fees before booking and are easy to use, making them the best option for taxis.</cite>
transport.taxis: <cite index="45-37,45-38,45-40,45-41">Madrid's 15,000 official taxis are white cars with diagonal red bands and are safe and reasonably reliable, though language barriers may require Google Translate.</cite> <cite index="48-4,48-18">Fixed airport rate of €33 to any location within central Madrid's M-30 ring, with 2026 rates starting at €2.55 minimum fare during weekdays.</cite>
transport.tips: <cite index="41-4,41-5,50-10,50-18">Madrid is very safe but crowded areas like Sol or the Metro attract pickpockets - keep bags zipped and in front, avoid back pockets, and never leave belongings unattended.</cite>

customs: <cite index="76-18,76-19,76-20,76-21">Greeting customs: Use 'dos besos' (two light kisses on cheeks starting with right) for friends/acquaintances, handshakes for formal meetings or between men unless they're close.</cite>
<cite index="71-5,76-26,76-27">Late dining schedule: Lunch is the main meal at 2-3 PM, dinner at 9-10 PM, requiring adjustment from Northern European and American habits.</cite>

<cite index="71-10,71-11,71-12,71-14">Meal etiquette: Spanish dining emphasizes leisurely pace with 1-2 hour meals, bread accompanies all courses, wine/beer is common but moderation prevails over intoxication.</cite>

health.overview: <cite index="66-8,66-9,66-10">Spain's public healthcare system (Sistema Nacional de Salud) provides universal healthcare with many services free of charge, covering general medicine, family practice, pediatrics, nursing, and physical therapy.</cite> <cite index="1-14,1-15">Medical help is available anywhere in the country, though it might take longer in smaller cities for assistance to arrive.</cite>
health.water: Tap water in Madrid is safe to drink and of excellent quality. Bottled water is widely available but unnecessary from a health perspective.
health.vaccinations: <cite index="1-31,3-3,3-36">No vaccinations are required for travel to Spain from the US.</cite> Standard travel vaccinations like hepatitis A/B may be recommended by your doctor.
health.altitude: <cite index="1-20,1-21,1-22">Madrid experiences intense summer heatwaves with temperatures exceeding 104°F in some regions - take proper precautions when exercising outdoors and stay hydrated.</cite> No altitude concerns as Madrid sits at 2,100 feet elevation.

emergency.usEmbassy: <cite index="52-1,55-19">US Embassy Madrid: +34 91 587 2200 for emergencies and general inquiries.</cite> Located at Calle de Serrano, 75, 28006 Madrid.

faq.a: <cite index="16-30,16-31">Madrid is one of Europe's safest capital cities with violent crime being rare - most travelers are much more likely to deal with pickpockets or minor scams than anything serious.</cite> <cite index="14-2,14-30">It's considered one of the safest megacities in the world compared to other cities with millions of inhabitants.</cite>

<cite index="20-3,20-4">Yes, Madrid is safe at night especially compared to other major capitals, with violent crime rare and the city center full of life.</cite> <cite index="20-15,20-16">The lively atmosphere contributes to safety as you're rarely alone on streets, even late at night, which tends to deter certain types of crime.</cite>

faq.q: Is Madrid safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: <cite index="31-1,31-2,38-5,38-16">Yes, Madrid is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers - described as 'the safest city I've ever been to' by experienced female solo travelers.</cite> <cite index="38-21,38-27,38-30">While occasional catcalling occurs, it's generally less menacing than other countries with no following, threats, or aggressive behavior.</cite>

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Madrid?
faq.a: <cite index="13-19,13-20">Villaverde in southern Madrid has higher incident rates due to social and economic challenges, though serious violent crime remains limited.</cite> <cite index="14-12">Some neighborhoods like Puente de Vallecas are mentioned as areas requiring extra caution.</cite> Central tourist areas are very safe but watch for pickpockets in crowded spots like Sol and Gran Vía.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Madrid?
faq.a: <cite index="50-25,50-26,41-35">Madrid's public transport is considered one of the best in Europe - clean, reliable, very safe, and the Metro is the fastest and most reliable option.</cite> <cite index="41-4,41-5">Main safety concern is pickpockets in crowded areas - keep bags zipped and in front, avoid back pockets for phones/wallets.</cite>
